recovery.name: Recovery & Sobriety
recovery.viewer_persona: A man in his late 30s to early 50s, in active recovery or struggling to stay sober. He is often awake at 2am not from cravings, but from the feelings sobriety uncovered that alcohol used to bury. He holds it together for his family. Nobody at work knows. He questions whether the flatness he feels will ever pass.

ai_companion.name: AI Companion
ai_companion.viewer_persona: A man in his 40s who lives in his head. He has people around him but feels fundamentally alone like he is performing a version of himself rather than living. He is curious about technology. He would never call a helpline. He would talk to an AI before he would talk to a friend about how he really feels.

mens_mental_health.name: Men's Mental Health
mens_mental_health.viewer_persona: A man mid-30s to late 40s who has been the strong one for so long he has forgotten what it feels like to not be. He is not in crisis. He is just exhausted in a way he cannot explain to anyone. He would not use the word depressed. He would say he is not himself lately.

mood_wellness.name: Mood & General Wellness
mood_wellness.viewer_persona: A man in his 30s to 40s who notices patterns in himself but has no framework to understand them. He is functional. He goes to the gym, does his job, looks fine from the outside. But something is off and he cannot name it. He is the type to download an app instead of talking to someone.
